purpose: Measure small-model transport reliability and website workflow completion without changing epistemic acceptance semantics.
seed_semantics: Fixed replicate identifier and Python-local seed. The current frozen Route has no provider sampling-seed field, so this is not a claim of provider-level determinism.

protocol.equal_budget_rule: Every prompt/profile/seed trial receives the same cycles and token budget. A shakedown subset is never acceptance-eligible.
protocol.registration_order: profile order, then prompt id order, then seed order
protocol.retry_rule: Use only the harness-bounded repair and ordinary scheduler cycles; the evaluator adds no model retry.
protocol.missing_data_rule: Interrupted or absent trials produce insufficient_evidence, never an imputed success or failure.
protocol.multiple_comparison_rule: A3 and A4 use the minimum observed hot-path role rate; A9 requires every locked frontier metric to remain within bound.

A6.metric: complete typed terminal diagnostic rate among design-terminal failures

A7.metric: invalid manifest transitions into post-validation stages

A8.metric: full existing test suite local-execution verification

A9.metric: locked frontier metric regression against eligible baseline

A10.metric: frontier compact calls without a logged recovery transition

frontier_baseline.missing_baseline_policy: A9 is insufficient_evidence; never infer a baseline from candidate results.

prompts.prompt: Create a single-file museum website about the wonders of DNA with a hero, discovery timeline, replication explainer, gene-expression section, ethics panel, and references. Use only inline HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Build an artistic one-page deep-ocean observatory with six visually distinct sections, scroll-linked depth transitions, animated species cards, and a live-looking pressure gauge.

Design a climate-resilient city guide with map-like districts, project cards, a comparison table, glossary, and action checklist. Motion may enrich navigation but the reduced-motion version must remain complete.

Create a polished astronomy field guide with a constellation viewer, observing calendar, equipment chooser, and note panel. An approved project animation library may be requested only if native browser APIs are insufficient.
Make a responsive public-transport explainer with a route hero, service-status cards, fare calculator mockup, accessibility information, and a compact network diagram, all without runtime packages.

Produce a cinematic history of computing from relays to neuromorphic chips, using modular eras, an interactive architecture comparison, strong scroll motion, and a fully usable non-animated presentation.
Build one focused pomodoro timer panel with task text, start-pause-reset controls, a circular progress display, keyboard support, and no external assets or packages.
Create one self-contained interactive DNA double-helix hero that responds to pointer movement, explains base pairing in concise labels, and animates smoothly on modern browsers.
Design one accessible medication schedule card with time slots, taken toggles, clear status feedback, print styling, and a reduced-motion experience with no loss of information.

Build one rich but compact audio waveform explorer with playhead, selection range, annotations, and keyboard controls. It may request one approved project library if its exact capability is justified.
Make one responsive mortgage comparison widget with three scenarios, sliders, monthly-payment output, an amortisation summary, and all computation running locally without dependencies.
Create one tabbed field-notes component whose supplied legacy examples all use id=panel. Correct the collision by assigning unique owned DOM identifiers while preserving the intended tab interaction.
Create an eye-catching 2026-style journey through DNA replication with parallax layers, sticky narrative beats, animated molecular machinery, scroll-driven transitions, and clear scientific captions.

Build a kinetic typography site explaining gravitational waves. Use native CSS and browser APIs only, with wave interference, reveal transitions, and responsive motion that never hides the core explanation.

Design a virtual modern-dance programme with an animated stage map, performer trails, scene transitions, and programme notes. An approved animation dependency may be requested if lifecycle cleanup is explicit.
Create a dramatic volcanic-eruption explainer with a magma cross-section, particle-like ash, scroll-linked pressure build-up, hazard cards, and an evacuation summary.
Build an animated three-scene product story. The legacy brief repeats ids hero and canvas in every scene; namespace all ownership correctly while retaining seamless transitions.

Produce a scroll-driven coral-reef story. A stale brief says the bleaching chart depends on a component named ocean-engine that does not exist; resolve the dependency safely and deliver a coherent page.
Create an animated migraine-aura education page whose reduced-motion mode removes drifting, flashing, and parallax effects while preserving every explanation and interaction.
Build a disaster-preparedness guide with animated risk levels, collapsible plans, supply tracking, and a complete static fallback suitable for vestibular-sensitive users and printing.
Design a local-only breathing exercise page with optional paced animation, prominent pause controls, no auto-start, no external assets, and an equally informative static timing guide.
Make an interactive geological-timescale page with zoom and transitions. If one approved dependency is requested, it must support teardown and the reduced-motion path must not initialize it.
Create one carousel for an oral-history archive with manual controls, readable transcripts, no autoplay in reduced-motion mode, and a stable static list fallback.
Build a sensory-friendly school timetable from legacy cards that repeat id=details. Correct ownership collisions and ensure all state changes remain understandable without animation.
Create a privacy-first personal finance dashboard with budgets, transaction examples, goals, and small inline charts. Use no runtime imports, CDNs, remote fonts, or network requests.
Build a vivid photosynthesis explainer with leaf layers, photon motion, and a carbon-cycle sequence using only native HTML, CSS, SVG, and JavaScript.
Make one offline-capable colour-contrast explorer with foreground and background inputs, WCAG-style ratio feedback, sample text, and no external code.
Design an accessible earthquake-wave comparison with optional native animation and a static side-by-side diagram. No imports or remote resources are allowed.
Create a local recipe organiser. The input brief references a missing pantry-service component; remove that stale dependency and implement the intended filter locally without packages.
Build an offline election-method explainer whose three supplied examples each call their result region id=result. Namespace them correctly and avoid every external dependency.

Create a premium scroll narrative about the birth of a star. Native APIs are preferred, but one approved animation engine may be requested for a named capability with explicit mount and destroy behavior.
Build a data-story site about global migration with an overview, flow visualization, regional profiles, and methodology. Request only approved project dependencies that are genuinely necessary.
Create one interactive network graph for a fictional research team. It may use a single approved graph library if the required exports and cleanup path are declared precisely.
Design a smooth-scrolling architecture portfolio. Any approved scroll coordinator must be optional, bounded, torn down cleanly, and omitted entirely for reduced-motion users.
Build an interactive genomics atlas. A legacy note requests chart-core-v0, which is unavailable; select only a permitted capability or implement the chart natively without retaining the stale alias.
Create a gallery with an approved transition library if justified. Correct the supplied duplicate gallery-root ids across overview and detail components and declare unambiguous lifecycle ownership.

Build a community-garden planner with plots, crop cards, weather guidance, and tasks. The brief says tasks depends on forecast-widget, but only weather-guide exists; resolve the reference rather than inventing a hidden component.
Create an animated supply-chain explainer. A stale arrow layer calls an export named moveCargo that no component declares; repair the interface explicitly or remove the call.
Make an offline language-learning page. Its old score panel references remote-progress and syncScore, neither of which exists; redesign the local interaction with a valid dependency graph.
Build a reduced-motion-friendly transit disruption guide. The alert component names an absent map-controller dependency; retain the useful alert behavior without an unknown alias.
Create a microscopy image-story. A legacy manifest mentions obsolete zoom-kit and an undeclared focus export; use only a permitted dependency contract or a native implementation with valid wiring.
Build one standalone quiz card. The supplied notes incorrectly say it depends on question-bank; make it genuinely standalone and keep its export surface minimal.
Create a conference microsite from a legacy mockup where header, schedule, speakers, and venue all claim id=section. Assign deterministic unique ownership while preserving cross-section navigation.
Build a four-chapter animated space mission story. The old prototypes reuse id=scene and id=canvas in each chapter; namespace every DOM resource and animation lifecycle.
Make a dependency-free civic budget explorer with three charts whose supplied SVGs all use id=gradient. Correct every collision without changing the intended visual palette.
Create an accessible museum tour where each stop's legacy card repeats id=transcript and id=media. Use unique ownership and a complete reduced-motion, text-first path.
Design a product comparison with optional approved charting. Both product panels currently claim chart-root and tooltip; isolate their identifiers, exports, and teardown behavior.
Build one nested accordion from legacy markup that repeats id=content on every row. Repair the ids, aria-controls links, and keyboard behavior within one owned component.

Create a mobile-first wildfire information hub with current-condition examples, preparation steps, evacuation routes, and recovery resources. It must remain clear at 320px and use no external resources.
Build one responsive periodic-table explorer that becomes a readable categorized list on narrow screens and communicates state changes without relying on motion.
Design an editorial data story about housing supply with a narrative, small charts, definitions, and source notes. Use an approved chart dependency only if its exact benefit exceeds native SVG.
Create a responsive weather-systems explainer with animated fronts and pressure fields on large screens, plus a complete static diagram sequence for small or reduced-motion contexts.
Build a responsive online exhibition whose inherited room templates reuse id=artwork and id=caption. Correct the collisions and preserve deep-link-style navigation between rooms.
Create a responsive food-systems dashboard. A discarded supplier-map component is still named as a dependency by the summary; remove or replace that edge with explicit valid wiring.
Build a local trip-planning prototype with destination cards, a day-by-day itinerary, budget summary, and clear empty/error states, entirely in one offline HTML file.
Create one kanban board component with keyboard-accessible cards, filtering, undo feedback, and optional use of one approved drag capability with explicit cleanup.
Design an interactive music-theory lesson with animated chord construction, quizzes, persistent visible feedback, and a fully equivalent static reduced-motion mode.
Build a study planner with courses, sessions, progress, and reminders. The old progress panel calls missing calendar-sync; implement valid local state flow without that undeclared export.
Create a multi-step application form whose copied steps all use id=form-title and id=error. Correct ownership, labels, focus movement, and summary behavior.

Build an interactive biodiversity dashboard with filters, species details, and habitat transitions. Any approved dependency must be bounded and removable; reduced-motion mode must preserve all filtering and content.
